core_thesis: Coomaraswamy holds that art is properly an intellectual and sacramental operation: making is a mode of knowing, and beauty names the shining-forth of form grounded in supra-individual truth rather than private expression. His comparative method accordingly treats Hindu and Buddhist doctrines not as local curiosities but as exact statements of a metaphysics of participation, symbol, and liberation ([The Transformation of Nature in Art, ch. 1](cite://coomaraswamy/the-transformation-of-nature-in-art/1); [Hinduism and Buddhism, II](cite://coomaraswamy/hinduism-and-buddhism/2)).
